info.name: Contact List App — QA Portfolio
info.description:
Full API test suite covering authentication, CRUD operations, contract validation, security (BOLA) testing, and negative scenarios. Tokens and IDs chain automatically between requests — run folders in order using the Collection Runner.

The email addresses are intentionally obscure - this is a shared public API and realistic looking emails risk conflicting with another tester's data.

request.description: Attempts to register a user with special characters and symbols as first and last names. The API performs no input validation on the firstName & lastName fields and creates the account successfully, returning 201. Expected behaviour: 400 with a name validation error.

item.name: [NEGATIVE][KNOWN BUG] Update User Email Already In Use

exec: // KNOWN BUG: API returns raw MongoError instead of a sanitised error message.
// Documented in README. Test currently validates actual (buggy) behaviour.
// Expected behaviour: 400 with { message: "Email address is already in use" }
pm.test("Error is Duplicate Key for Email", function () {

request.description: Attempts to update User 1's email to one already registered by another account. The API correctly returns 400, but instead of a handled error message it exposes a raw MongoDB duplicate key error in the response body, leaking internal database implementation details. This constitutes an improper error handling bug and a potential information disclosure vulnerability

request.description: Verifies the update user request worked correctly and the old credentials are no longer valid

item.name: Negative Tests - Logged out

request.description: Log out the user in preparation for next test

Attempts to retrieve the current user's profile without an authentication token. Verifies that the API correctly rejects unauthenticated requests with a 401 response, confirming that user data is not accessible without a valid session.

item.name: [NEGATIVE] Logged Out - Get Contact by ID

item.description: Testing to see that the invalidated token is correctly rejected

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] NoSQL injection does not bypass login", function () {
pm.test("[SECURITY] No token returned on injection attempt", function () {

request.description: Sends a NoSQL injection payload in the password field (`{"$gt": ""}`) attempting to bypass authentication. The API correctly rejects the attempt with a 401, confirming the login endpoint sanitises input and is not vulnerable to this attack vector

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] Server not vulnerable to $where injection", function () {
pm.test("[SECURITY] Response time not delayed by injection", function () {

request.description: Sends a MongoDB `$where` operator in the request body attempting to execute arbitrary JavaScript on the database. The API correctly rejects the payload, confirming this operator is not evaluated

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] NoSQL operator rejected in firstName field", function () {
pm.test("[SECURITY] Rejection is due to type validation not explicit sanitisation", function () {
pm.test("[SECURITY] Contact was not created", function () {

request.description: Sends a MongoDB operator (`{"$gt": ""}`) as a field value when adding a contact, attempting to inject malicious data into the database. The API correctly handles the input and stores it as a plain string rather than evaluating it as a query operator

pm.test("[SECURITY] Response is still 200 but scoped to User 2 only", function () {

request.description: First step of the BOLA (Broken Object Level Authorisation) attack sequence. User 2 attempts to retrieve all contacts belonging to User 1 by using User 1's contact ID. Tests that the API correctly isolates data between users

item.name: [SECURITY] User 2 reads User 1's contact by ID

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] User 2 cannot access User 1's contact by ID", function () {
// Should return 403 Forbidden or 404 Not Found — NOT 200
pm.test("[SECURITY] Response does not contain User 1's contact data", function () {

request.description: User 2 attempts to retrieve a specific contact belonging to User 1 by ID. Tests that the API enforces object-level authorisation and does not expose another user's data

item.name: [SECURITY][VULNERABILITY] User 2 accesses account using User 1's token

exec: pm.test("[VULNERABILITY] API incorrectly accepts another user's token - returns 200", function () {
pm.test("[VULNERABILITY] API returns user data - token not scoped to authenticated user", function () {

request.description: The API accepts User 1's token when called in the context of User 2's session, indicating token validation is not scoped to the authenticated user. This constitutes a broken authentication vulnerability

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] User 2 cannot update User 1's contact", function () {
pm.test("[SECURITY] firstName was not changed to HACKED", function () {

request.description: User 2 attempts to modify a contact belonging to User 1. Tests that write operations enforce object-level authorisation and cannot be exploited to tamper with another user's data

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] User 2 cannot delete User 1's contact", function () {

request.description: User 2 attempts to delete a contact belonging to User 1. Tests that destructive operations enforce object-level authorisation and cannot be used to destroy another user's data

item.name: [SECURITY] Verify User 1's contact still exists after attack

exec: pm.test("[SECURITY] User 1's contact was not deleted or modified", function () {
pm.test("[SECURITY] Contact data is intact", function () {

request.description: Integrity check — confirms User 1's contact was not modified or deleted during the BOLA attack sequence. Logs back in as User 1 and retrieves the contact by ID to verify the data is intact

exec: pm.execution.setNextRequest('[SECURITY][VULNERABILITY] Brute Force Login'); // loops back to itself
pm.test('FINDING: No rate limiting after 10 attempts', () => {
// API returns 401 every time — 429 never triggered
// In production this endpoint should implement rate limiting

request.description: When using the collection runner, it sends 10 consecutive failed login attempts using incorrect passwords. The API returns 401 on every attempt without triggering any rate limiting, account lockout, or CAPTCHA challenge, leaving the API exposed to brute force attacks
